---
title: "Prompt-injection classifier — methodology submission"
author: "Brandon Behring"
date: "2026-05-19"
description: "Headline finding + plain-language interpretation + 3 drill-down links. Restructured v1.1.1 per ADR-061."
---

# Prompt-injection classifier — methodology submission

This is the landing page. **Headline result is up-top; plain-language
meaning is right below; three obvious drill-down links lead to the
full content.** The full reading guide (3 paths + 14 headline ADRs +
repo map) lives at [`READING_GUIDE.md`](READING_GUIDE.md) — one click
away if you want it.

The submission is a methodology + capability characterisation of a
prompt-injection classifier rung ladder, evaluated under an honest
OOD slate. **No rung is promoted as a deployment recommendation** —
each rung's trade-offs are characterised; readers with a specific
deployment context map our characterisation onto their cost constraints.

---

## Headline finding

**The OOD wall is cross-family, not cross-source.** Training pool is 4
direct-injection sources; the 5-slice OOD test slate probes attack
families *absent* from training (indirect injection, multi-turn
agentic-flow, jailbreaks, benign-but-injection-shaped). Direct-injection
training performance does not translate.

`pooled_ood` AUPRC trio (BCa bootstrap, 10000 resamples):

| Rung | `pooled_ood` AUPRC | 95% CI | vs prevalence baseline (0.374) |
|---|---|---|---|
| ModernBERT frozen-probe | **0.364** | [0.354, 0.375] | below baseline (CI upper bound just touches it) |
| TF-IDF + LR (classical floor) | 0.291 | [0.283, 0.298] | far below |
| ModernBERT LoRA | 0.293 | [0.286, 0.301] | far below; -0.071 vs frozen-probe (CI clears zero) |
| ProtectAI v1 (reference) | 0.361 | [0.330, 0.391] | below baseline (CI upper bound clears it) |
| ProtectAI v2 (reference) | 0.314 | [0.283, 0.345] | far below; v2 does NOT monotonically improve over v1 |

---

## What this means in plain language

1. **No rung clears the random-guessing floor on OOD attacks.** The
   prevalence baseline (0.374) is what a coin flip would score on this
   slice. The best trained rung (frozen-probe at 0.364) lands just
   below it. **This is the load-bearing negative result.**

2. **Fine-tuning made things worse, not better.** LoRA scored -0.071
   AUPRC vs the frozen-probe on the OOD slate (statistically clear).
   Training on direct-injection examples burns the OOD generalisation
   budget that lives in the pretrained backbone.

3. **OOD here means "different attack family," not "different source."**
   The slate tests indirect injection (BIPIA), multi-turn agents
   (InjecAgent), jailbreaks (JBB-Behaviors), and benign-but-injection-
   shaped texts (NotInject, XSTest). The wall is at the attack-family
   boundary; in-source held-out direct-injection performance is fine.

4. **Off-the-shelf detector updates can regress.** ProtectAI v2 beats
   v1 on jailbreaks (+0.037 AUPRC) but loses on benign-injection-shaped
   text (-0.087 AUPRC). Version monotonicity is not guaranteed across
   OOD slices.

5. **The "tune on val, ship to prod" recipe under-quantifies real
   FPR.** Thresholds tuned on the validation slice produce 1-12 %
   detection FPRs on LODO test data. Project response: dual-policy
   threshold characterisation (detection FPR ≤ 1 % + verification
   recall ≥ 99 %), both reported with reachability flags.

For the technical version of each pattern with statistical detail,
see [`READING_GUIDE.md` §How to read the headline numbers](READING_GUIDE.md#how-to-read-the-headline-numbers--5-interpretation-patterns).

---

## Drill deeper

- **Full results + figures**: [`RESULTS.md`](RESULTS.md) — 5-rung ×
  5-slice AUPRC + AUROC + recall@FPR1% grid + 7 figures + raw-data
  pointers. Includes the v1.0.9 §1B DeBERTa-v3-base ablation appendix
  placeholder (methodology locked at v1.1.0; execution v1.1.1).
- **Full methodology**: [`WRITEUP.md`](WRITEUP.md) — cover narrative
  (hub) linking out to 8 detailed-spoke pages on data, rungs,
  evaluation, thresholds, reference scorers, methodology guarantees,
  limitations, reproducibility.
- **How to read this site**: [`READING_GUIDE.md`](READING_GUIDE.md) —
  3 named reading paths (A1 quick-skim / A2 audit / A3 reproduce),
  14 headline ADRs, repo TOC, submission anchors.

---

## Submission anchors

- **Source pin**: [`tree/v1.0.0`](https://github.com/brandon-behring/prompt-injection-detection-prototype/tree/v1.0.0) (never drifts; reviewer URL pin).
- **Live site**: this page (reflects latest patch — currently v1.1.1).
- **Releases**: [`releases/`](https://github.com/brandon-behring/prompt-injection-detection-prototype/releases) (CHANGELOG + tag artifacts).
- **HF Hub model cards**: [`frozen-probe`](https://huggingface.co/BBehring/prompt-injection-frozen-probe) + [`lora`](https://huggingface.co/BBehring/prompt-injection-lora).
